LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GOSU, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IN PENDING ACTION UNTIL ARBITRATION COMPLETED. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT GOSU, LLC'S "MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IN PENDING ACTION UNTIL ARBITRATION IS COMPLETED" IS DENIED. NO APPLICATION HAS BEEN MADE FOR AN ORDER TO ARBITRATE, SO NO ARBITRATION EXISTS TO BE "COMPLETED." (SEE CCP 1281.4.) IF GOSU BELIEVES ANY ISSUE IS SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION, IT IS FREE TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL AND STAY. (SEE CCP 1292.8.) TO GRANT THE PENDING MOTION NOW WOULD BAR PLAINTIFF'S CASE INDEFINITELY WITH NO ADJUDICATION. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS AND REPRESENTATIVE ACTION SETTLEMENT AND PROVISIONAL CERTIFICATION OF CLASS. HEARING HELD AND THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PRELIMINARY APPROVAL IS GRANTED. PAYMENT TO CLASS COUNSEL FOR UP TO ONE-THIRD OF THE GROSS SETTLEMENT AMOUNT AND COSTS UP TO $20,000 WILL BASED ON WHAT IS PROVIDED WITH THE MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL PAPERS. THE PROPOSED TIMELINE IS APPROVED. COUNSEL IS TO REVISE THE NOTICE APPROPRIATELY AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT ALONG WITH THE PROPOSED ORDER THAT ALSO STATES THE FINAL APPROVAL PAPERS ARE TO INCLUDE DOCUMENTATION FOR LODESTAR AND ITEMIZATION FOR ALL COSTS. THE COURT RESERVES NOVEMBER 6, 2025 AT 9:00 AM FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR FINAL APPROVAL. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class And Representative Action Settlement And Provisional Certification Of Class
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS AND REPRESENTATIVE ACTION SETTLEMENT AND PROVISIONAL CERTIFICATION OF CLASS

Appearances: Nida Farishta of the Otkupman Law Firm (818.293.5623) for plaintiff ad moving party; Jose Macias of Macias Rodriguez LLP (408.455.1243) for defendant and responding party.

Hearing held and the court rules as follows: preliminary approval is granted. Payment to class counsel for up to one-third of the gross settlement amount and costs up to $20,000 will based on what is provided with the motion for final approval papers. The proposed timeline is approved. Counsel is to revise the notice appropriately and submit it to the court along with the proposed order that also states the final approval papers are to include documentation for lodestar and itemization for all costs. The court reserves November 6, 2025 at 9:00 am for plaintiff's motion for final approval. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Is Completed
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GOSU, LLC's Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Is Completed

Although this motion is unopposed, the court considers it opposed pursuant to the opposition filed in connection with the petition to compel arbitration. For the reasons stated in connection with that petition, this motion is denied.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Curtis E. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)

Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GOSU, LLC's Petition To Compel Arbitration

Denied. (1) The agreement to arbitrate is not signed by both parties and accordingly there is no evidence it represents their agreement. (2) (A) The agreement is procedurally unconscionable, because (i) it was a contract of adhesion, (ii) plaintiff had virtually no time to sign it, and (iii) it was a condition of employment. (B) The contract is substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's citation to OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 129 is not well-taken because that is a discussion of procedural, not substantive, unconscionability. However, Murrey v. Superior Court (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 1223, 1250 does refer to a "mix of unclear and contradictory rules" as evidence of substantive uncountability, and that is present here. The agreement at 3 is incoherent is its mandate that discovery be handled as if the case were in court "or" according to AAA rules; and further confusion is generated because both JAMS and AAA are cited as the fora governing the arbitration. The combination of procedural and substantive unconscionability renders the agreement unconscionable and unenforceable.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Curtis E. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

RE: Defendant GOSU, LLC's petition to compel arbitration.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant GOSU, LLC's petition to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice. The arbitration agreement which Defendant seeks to enforce is written in Spanish. California Rules of Court 3.1110(g) requires that "[e]xhibits written in a foreign language must be accompanied by an English translation, certified under oath by a qualified interpreter." Although Defendant claims Exhibit B of the petition is the English version (Chen Decl. at p. 6, 4), no such Exhibit B is attached to this petition to compel arbitration, Chen Declaration, or motion to stay proceedings. Even if an English copy is included in a prior filing, it would not cure the deficiency of the present motion and petition. Defendant's petition to compel arbitration and motion to stay proceedings are therefore denied without prejudice.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)

Motion For Order To Stay Proceeding In Pending Action Until Arbitration Is Completed
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

RE: Defendant GOSU, LLC's motion for order to stay proceedings.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant GOSU, LLC's motion for order to stay proceedings is denied. The arbitration agreement which Defendant seeks to enforce is written in Spanish. California Rules of Court 3.1110(g) requires that "[e]xhibits written in a foreign language must be accompanied by an English translation, certified under oath by a qualified interpreter." Although Defendant claims Exhibit B of the petition is the English version (Chen Decl. at p. 6, 4), no such Exhibit B is attached to this petition to compel arbitration, Chen Declaration, or motion to stay proceedings. Even if an English copy is included in a prior filing, it would not cure the deficiency of the present motion and petition. Defendant's petition to compel arbitration and motion to stay proceedings are therefore denied without prejudice.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed,
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant GOSU, LLC's "motion to stay proceedings in pending action until arbitration is completed".

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendant GOSU, LLC's "motion to stay proceedings in pending action until arbitration is completed" is denied. No application has been made for an order to arbitrate, so no arbitration exists to be "completed." (See CCP 1281.4.) If GOSU believes any issue is subject to arbitration, it is free to file a motion to compel and stay. (See CCP 1292.8.) To grant the pending motion now would bar plaintiff's case indefinitely with no adjudication.
Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
